one_line_takeaway: FRPO turns RLHF into a max-min over a KL neighborhood -- an entropic-risk tweak to GRPO with no added compute -- so that safety guardrails (and math accuracy) survive ordinary downstream fine-tuning, cutting safety erosion and beating GRPO by 22 points on post-code-tuning MATH.
key_finding: Baking KL-neighborhood reward-flatness into the base policy at RLHF time substantially slows safety-guardrail degradation under downstream SFT/RL, and preserves task capability under continual learning, at no extra training cost versus GRPO. For safety, lambda=0.2 best preserves the safety reward and yields the flattest reward landscape for both Mistral and Qwen, maintaining the highest HarmBench refusal rates and matching Llama-3-RR on StrongREJECT, while Table 1 shows downstream task scores comparable to GRPO (so the safety gains are not just weaker learning). For math continual learning, FRPO with lambda=2 preserves MATH500 accuracy best, outperforming GRPO by 22 points (64.5 vs 42.3 after code fine-tuning) while matching coding gains. The optimal lambda tracks the reward standard deviation (~0.05 for safety -> lambda~0.2; ~0.5 for math -> lambda~2), matching the std(r)/sqrt(2*rho) prediction, so lambda can be set without a sweep.
core_contribution: The paper introduces Fine-tuning Robust Policy Optimization (FRPO), a robust RLHF training framework that makes a base policy resistant to catastrophic forgetting of learned behaviors (notably safety guardrails) under later downstream fine-tuning. Instead of maximizing reward only at the current policy (as GRPO does), FRPO maximizes the worst-case reward over the set of all policies Q reachable within an average-KL ball of radius rho around the base policy pi_theta -- a max-min formulation. Using an f-divergence dual (Lemma 3.1 with f(x)=x log x, giving forward KL), the authors show this max-min problem is equivalent to optimizing an entropic-risk objective, -lambda * log E_pi[exp(-r/lambda)], where a tunable lambda controls sensitivity to low-reward trajectories and lambda -> infinity exactly recovers GRPO. They derive FRPO as a GRPO-style policy gradient with a closed-form partition-function estimate, a variance-cancelling baseline, and a jackknife bias correction, adding no extra computation over GRPO. The framing is 'pre-finetuning robustness': the base model itself should avoid brittle high-reward solutions in sharp regions of policy space, rather than relying on downstream-time interventions.
threat_model: The concern is catastrophic forgetting of previously-learned safety guardrails when a released/aligned base model is later fine-tuned on an ostensibly unrelated downstream task. The paper follows the fine-tuning-attack literature (Qi et al. 2023/2024) showing that ordinary SFT on benign datasets (Alpaca, GSM8K) can silently strip safety alignment and raise attack success rates. FRPO's assumption is that downstream adaptation stays within a bounded KL neighborhood of the base policy (empirically per-token KL in [0.02, 0.3] before overfitting), reflecting standard practice: KL-regularized RL, small learning rates, early stopping, or LoRA. Importantly, this is NOT an adversary who deliberately optimizes to maximize harm with unbounded budget -- FRPO is agnostic to the downstream objective but only robust to shifts that remain inside the KL ball. It contrasts itself with adversarial-fine-tuning defenses like TAR (iterative inner-loop attack search), Circuit Breaking / Representation Rerouting (unlearning / representation manipulation), and Representation Noising, which target explicitly malicious fine-tuning but are expensive or safety-specific.
limitations: Robustness is only guaranteed for downstream shifts inside the assumed average-KL ball; the method is explicitly not aimed at unbounded adversarial fine-tuning that maximizes harm (that regime is left to TAR-style defenses). The mean-variance trade-off and the optimal-lambda formula come from a large-lambda Taylor approximation, so the guidance is heuristic at small lambda. lambda must be matched to the reward std, which differs by task (0.2 for safety vs 2 for math). For the code continual-learning experiment, full-parameter SFT caused a substantial MATH500 drop, forcing the authors to use LoRA (rank 16, lr 5e-5) -- so the demonstrated preservation is under a constrained fine-tuning setup. Main safety experiments use only 7B-scale models (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, Mistral-v0.1-Instruct, both chosen for weak native guardrails), and the SAM ablation uses SAM's default perturbation radius rather than a tuned one. Several supporting results (RL-on-UltraFeedback trade-off, replay comparison, broader GSM8K baselines) are deferred to appendices.

datasets_used: Safety training set: 1000 harmful + 1000 harmless prompts (custom)
GSM8K (downstream SFT and math forgetting probe)
MATH levels 3-5 / MATH500 (math RL training + eval)
quantitative_results: FRPO(lambda=2) preserves MATH500 after code fine-tuning at 64.5% vs GRPO 42.3% -- a 22-point (22%) advantage
All math-trained models reach ~73% on MATH500 after math training, up from Qwen2.5-Math-7B base of 56.6%
MBPP+ improves ~4-5% for all models after code fine-tuning (base ~57.4% -> FT ~62%); FRPO(lambda=2) 57.9 base -> 62.4 FT

Table 1 (Mistral-FRPO lambda=0.2, Base->FT after GSM8K SFT): GSM8K 48.7->56.2, MMLU 55.2->54.2, IFEval 36.6->37.0, HumanEval 31.1->24.4 -- comparable to Mistral-GRPO (48.4->55.5, 54.9->54.1, 34.4->38.0, 32.3->24.4)
Empirical per-token KL under normal fine-tuning falls in [0.02, 0.3]; helpfulness collapses beyond KL~0.3 (overfitting onset), motivating rho at KL=0.3
Predicted optimal lambda* in [0.09, 0.35] for the KL range, containing the chosen lambda=0.2; reward std ~0.07 (safety) end of training
Optimal lambda scales with reward std: ~0.05 std -> lambda~0.2 (safety); ~0.5 std -> lambda~2 (math)
48-setting fine-tuning sweep (LoRA rank 16/32, lr 1e-6..1e-4, 1-2 epochs, Alpaca/GSM8K): FRPO stays above GRPO across the range
All safety results averaged over 3 runs; math results averaged over 3 fine-tuning seeds
baselines_compared: GRPO (primary controlled baseline; recovered as lambda -> infinity)
GRPO + SAM (parameter-space sharpness-aware min, P=2, rho=0.05) -- improves over GRPO but stays below FRPO(lambda=0.2)
Mistral-RR and Llama-3-RR (Representation Rerouting / Circuit Breaking, unlearning)
Replay / rehearsal methods (deferred to appendix B.4)
